channel.title: Light Tower Group — NYC CRE Capital Markets Insights
channel.description: Data-driven capital markets analysis and building intelligence for NYC commercial real estate professionals.

item.title: Adam Driver's $5M Loft Sale: Celebrity Liquidity Meets Brooklyn's Narrow Bid
item.description: Adam Driver's Brooklyn loft went into contract in two weeks at nearly $5M. The speed reveals liquidity for trophy condos, but only at the right basis and location.

news:news.news:title: Adam Driver's $5M Loft Sale: Celebrity Liquidity Meets Brooklyn's Narrow Bid
news:news.news:keywords: Brooklyn, luxury condos, celebrity real estate, market liquidity, pricing

item.title: Brazil's Credit Push Tests the Line Between Fiscal Policy and Monetary Control
item.description: Brazil's Finance Minister says new credit lines won't affect monetary policy. For CRE capital markets, the real question is whether fiscal expansion forces rates higher.

news:news.news:title: Brazil's Credit Push Tests the Line Between Fiscal Policy and Monetary Control
news:news.news:keywords: Brazil, monetary policy, credit lines, fiscal policy, interest rates

item.title: A 168-Unit Bronx Filing Shows Development Is Alive Where the Math Works
item.description: Permits filed for 168 units at 78 West 180th Street in the Bronx. The filing signals that development capital is still available for transit-oriented, mid-scale projects in lower-cost submarkets.

news:news.news:title: A 168-Unit Bronx Filing Shows Development Is Alive Where the Math Works
news:news.news:keywords: Bronx development, New York City multifamily, development finance, transit-oriented development, construction permits

item.title: Bronx Lottery Reveals the Real Math Behind Affordable Housing Development
item.description: Westbridge Realty's 90-unit Bronx project shows how affordable housing development relies on subsidy, not market rent. 18 lottery units reveal the capital math.

news:news.news:title: Bronx Lottery Reveals the Real Math Behind Affordable Housing Development
news:news.news:keywords: affordable housing, Bronx, Westbridge Realty, capital stack, subsidy, development finance, New York City

item.title: Alexander Brothers' Jail Cell Phone Use Reveals a Capital Market Blind Spot
item.description: The Alexander brothers' alleged contraband phone use from jail reveals a deeper capital markets risk: sponsor control from behind bars, and what it means for lenders, investors, and asset recovery.

news:news.news:title: Alexander Brothers' Jail Cell Phone Use Reveals a Capital Market Blind Spot
news:news.news:keywords: sponsor risk, lender due diligence, asset recovery, capital markets, Alexander brothers

item.title: Trammell Crow's Soho Tower Shows Development Is Alive Only for Those With Cheap Land and Patient Capital
item.description: Trammell Crow's 130 Lafayette Street rises past halfway in Soho. A 99-year ground lease and $14.7M basis reveal the capital conditions required to build today.

news:news.news:title: Trammell Crow's Soho Tower Shows Development Is Alive Only for Those With Cheap Land and Patient Capital
news:news.news:keywords: Trammell Crow, Soho, development, ground lease, New York City, multifamily

item.title: Cord Meyer's White Plains Groundbreaking Shows Construction Debt Is Available for the Right Sponsor and Location
item.description: Cord Meyer Development and Saber Real Estate North break ground on 203-unit mixed-use project in White Plains, backed by a $96M ACRES construction loan. The deal shows development debt is available for experienced sponsors in infill locations.

news:news.news:title: Cord Meyer's White Plains Groundbreaking Shows Construction Debt Is Available for the Right Sponsor and Location
news:news.news:keywords: construction financing, Cord Meyer Development, White Plains, ACRES, multifamily development, Westchester County

item.title: Boston's Empty Lab Towers Show Speculative Capital Meets Its Match
item.description: Boston's 10 World Trade sits vacant as lab vacancy hits 32.7%. A $540M spec tower shows the gap between construction financing and tenant demand.

news:news.news:title: Boston's Empty Lab Towers Show Speculative Capital Meets Its Match
news:news.news:keywords: lab real estate, Boston Seaport, speculative development, vacancy, PGIM Real Estate, Wheelock Street Capital

item.title: Lender Forces Sale of Three Buildings: The Capital Discipline Behind the Headline
item.description: Community Stabilization Partners forces Ved Parkash to sell three troubled buildings and repair 21 others. The deal shows how private capital is enforcing accountability in rent-stabilized portfolios.

news:news.news:title: Lender Forces Sale of Three Buildings: The Capital Discipline Behind the Headline
news:news.news:keywords: Ved Parkash, Community Stabilization Partners, rent-stabilized, lender enforcement, New York City

item.title: The Croman Name Returns to Multifamily, But the Capital Thesis Has Changed
item.description: Jake and Adam Croman are buying rent-stabilized Manhattan walkups. The strategy signals a shift in how capital is pricing regulated housing risk post-2019.

news:news.news:title: The Croman Name Returns to Multifamily, But the Capital Thesis Has Changed
news:news.news:keywords: Manhattan multifamily, rent-stabilized, Croman, value-add, capital thesis

item.title: Summit Hotel's $650M Refi Shows Unsecured Debt Is Open for the Right Lodging Sponsors
item.description: Summit Hotel Properties refinanced and upsized its $650M unsecured credit facility. The deal shows banks are still lending to high-quality lodging REITs with scale and balance sheet credibility.

news:news.news:title: Summit Hotel's $650M Refi Shows Unsecured Debt Is Open for the Right Lodging Sponsors
news:news.news:keywords: Summit Hotel Properties, credit facility, refinancing, lodging REIT, unsecured debt, balance sheet

item.title: Ken Griffin's $125M Condo Buyout: The Real Price of Assembling a Corporate Campus
item.description: Ken Griffin spent $125M and three years secretly buying a Miami condo tower to demolish it for Citadel's campus. The premium paid reveals the cost of site control in a liquid market.

news:news.news:title: Ken Griffin's $125M Condo Buyout: The Real Price of Assembling a Corporate Campus
news:news.news:keywords: Ken Griffin, Citadel, Miami, Brickell, site assembly, condo buyout, development

item.title: Rithm's $73M Equity Check Reveals the Real Cost of Office Refinancing
item.description: Rithm Capital puts $73M equity into 31 W. 52nd St refinancing. The $415M CMBS loan reveals how office liquidity requires sponsor cash, not just asset quality.

news:news.news:title: Rithm's $73M Equity Check Reveals the Real Cost of Office Refinancing
news:news.news:keywords: Rithm Capital, Paramount Group, office refinancing, CMBS, Manhattan office

item.title: Azorim's Yonkers Loan Shows Construction Debt Is Open Only for Proven Sponsors
item.description: Azorim secures $68.75M construction loan from Western Alliance Bank for Miroza Tower 4 in Yonkers. The fixed-rate, interest-only structure reveals which developers can still access construction debt in 2026.

news:news.news:title: Azorim's Yonkers Loan Shows Construction Debt Is Open Only for Proven Sponsors
news:news.news:keywords: construction financing, Azorim, Western Alliance Bank, Walker & Dunlop, Yonkers development, multifamily

item.title: Brookfield's Canary Wharf Data Centers: The Real Play Is Energy, Not Real Estate
item.description: Brookfield plans AI data centers in Canary Wharf. The move reveals a capital strategy that prioritizes energy access and long-term contracts over traditional real estate returns.

news:news.news:title: Brookfield's Canary Wharf Data Centers: The Real Play Is Energy, Not Real Estate
news:news.news:keywords: Brookfield, data centers, Canary Wharf, AI infrastructure, energy demand, capital markets, real estate

item.title: AllianceBernstein's $75M Industrial Loan Shows Private Credit Is Picking the Right Development Risk
item.description: AllianceBernstein lends $75M for Rechler Equity's Long Island industrial park expansion. The deal signals private credit's appetite for low-leverage, pre-leased development with sponsor track record.

news:news.news:title: AllianceBernstein's $75M Industrial Loan Shows Private Credit Is Picking the Right Development Risk
news:news.news:keywords: AllianceBernstein, Rechler Equity Partners, industrial development, private credit, Long Island industrial, development financing, JLL

item.title: Starwood's $10.2B Fund Shows AI Infrastructure Is the New Core, Not a Side Bet
item.description: Starwood Capital closes $10.2B opportunistic fund with 35% data center allocation. The capital rotation into AI infrastructure is structural, not tactical.

news:news.news:title: Starwood's $10.2B Fund Shows AI Infrastructure Is the New Core, Not a Side Bet
news:news.news:keywords: Starwood Capital, data centers, AI infrastructure, opportunistic fund, capital rotation, Barry Sternlicht

item.title: Sonder's Collapse Exposes the Risk in Master-Lease CMBS Structures
item.description: A $131.5M CMBS loan on Moinian's 2 Washington Street hits special servicing after Sonder's bankruptcy. The master lease structure and short-term rental restrictions create a workout challenge.

news:news.news:title: Sonder's Collapse Exposes the Risk in Master-Lease CMBS Structures
news:news.news:keywords: CMBS, special servicing, master lease, Sonder, Moinian, New York City, distress

item.title: DWS REIT Redemption Cap Exposes the Liquidity Gap in Nontraded Structures
item.description: DWS Group's RREEF Property Trust missed 32.4% of June redemption requests, revealing the structural liquidity mismatch in nontraded REITs and investor pressure.

news:news.news:title: DWS REIT Redemption Cap Exposes the Liquidity Gap in Nontraded Structures
news:news.news:keywords: nontraded REIT, redemption, liquidity, DWS Group, RREEF Property Trust, capital markets

item.title: Natixis’s $193M Miami Loan Shows Construction Debt Is Open Only for the Right Sponsor and Basis
item.description: Natixis provides $192.5M construction loan for LCOR's 544-unit Miami tower. The deal reveals how construction debt is flowing only to sponsors with low basis and institutional credibility.

news:news.news:title: Natixis’s $193M Miami Loan Shows Construction Debt Is Open Only for the Right Sponsor and Basis
news:news.news:keywords: construction loan, Miami multifamily, Natixis, LCOR, IPA Capital Markets

item.title: Madison Realty's $61M Bridge Loan Shows Private Credit Is Still the Only Game for New Construction
item.description: Madison Realty Capital provides $61M bridge loan for 135-unit North Bergen apartment complex. Floating-rate debt on new construction shows private credit filling the gap left by banks.

news:news.news:title: Madison Realty's $61M Bridge Loan Shows Private Credit Is Still the Only Game for New Construction
news:news.news:keywords: Madison Realty Capital, bridge loan, private credit, multifamily, North Bergen, Walker & Dunlop, Sanz Property Management

item.title: Manhattan Office Rents Are Rising. The Capital Story Is More Complicated.
item.description: Manhattan office asking rents hit $78.03/SF, the highest since 2020. But the capital story is about bifurcation: who can finance, who can refinance, and who gets left behind.

news:news.news:title: Manhattan Office Rents Are Rising. The Capital Story Is More Complicated.
news:news.news:keywords: Manhattan office, rents, Colliers, leasing, capital markets, bifurcation, office market

item.title: CMBS Delinquency Rate Dips, But the Underlying Pressure Is Not Easing
item.description: Trepp CMBS delinquency rate fell 20 bps to 7.35% in June 2026, driven by a large lodging cure. But retail and multifamily rates rose, signaling uneven distress and narrow refinancing windows.

news:news.news:title: CMBS Delinquency Rate Dips, But the Underlying Pressure Is Not Easing
news:news.news:keywords: CMBS, delinquency, Trepp, retail, multifamily, lodging, office, distress

item.title: Recapitalizations Are the New Exit: Liquidity Without a Sale
item.description: As refinancing pressure mounts and exits stall, private real estate investors increasingly use recapitalizations to unlock liquidity and extend hold periods.

news:news.news:title: Recapitalizations Are the New Exit: Liquidity Without a Sale
news:news.news:keywords: recapitalization, liquidity, refinancing, private real estate, hold period

item.title: TPG's €400M European Medical Portfolio Play Is a Bet on Demographics, Not Real Estate
item.description: TPG Real Estate launches Arcura Medical Properties after a €400M acquisition of German and Dutch medical outpatient assets. The move reveals institutional appetite for demographic-driven, non-discretionary real estate income.

news:news.news:title: TPG's €400M European Medical Portfolio Play Is a Bet on Demographics, Not Real Estate
news:news.news:keywords: TPG Real Estate, Arcura Medical Properties, healthcare real estate, European CRE, medical outpatient, Vital Infrastructure Property Trust, Northwest Healthcare Properties REIT, institutional capital, demographics

item.title: Warsh's Inflation Warning Means CRE Owners Should Plan for Higher-for-Longer Rates
item.description: Fed Chair Warsh says inflation too high, no July rate signal. 30-year mortgage near 6.49%. CRE owners face prolonged higher-for-longer rates, compressing refinancing windows.

news:news.news:title: Warsh's Inflation Warning Means CRE Owners Should Plan for Higher-for-Longer Rates
news:news.news:keywords: Federal Reserve, interest rates, inflation, commercial real estate, refinancing risk, Kevin Warsh, monetary policy, CRE capital markets

item.title: Harvest Capital's $600M Florida Deal Shows Land Banking Is Back, but Only With Institutional Partners
item.description: Harvest Capital and TPG Credit close $600M recap for Metro Development Group's Florida master-planned communities, signaling institutional appetite for land banking.

news:news.news:title: Harvest Capital's $600M Florida Deal Shows Land Banking Is Back, but Only With Institutional Partners
news:news.news:keywords: Harvest Capital, TPG Credit, Metro Development Group, land banking, private credit, Florida development, master-planned communities, recapitalization, Sun Belt

item.title: Hayfin's €15 Billion Fund Shows Private Credit Is Concentrating, Not Growing
item.description: Hayfin raises €15B for direct lending, signaling institutional capital is concentrating with top firms. Mid-market CRE lenders face pressure as liquidity narrows.

news:news.news:title: Hayfin's €15 Billion Fund Shows Private Credit Is Concentrating, Not Growing
news:news.news:keywords: private credit, direct lending, Hayfin Capital Management, institutional capital, CRE debt

item.title: Harvest Capital-TPG's $600M Metro Deal Shows Land Financing Has Left the Banking System
item.description: Harvest Capital and TPG close $600M facility for Metro Development Group. The deal reveals how land and lot financing has migrated from banks to private credit platforms.

news:news.news:title: Harvest Capital-TPG's $600M Metro Deal Shows Land Financing Has Left the Banking System
news:news.news:keywords: Harvest Capital, TPG Credit, Metro Development Group, land financing, private credit, master-planned communities, Florida, residential development, nonrecourse debt, capital markets

item.title: Starwood's $10.2B Fund Shows Data Center Capital Is Too Big for Traditional CRE Allocations
item.description: Starwood Capital closes $10.2B opportunistic fund with 35% allocated to data centers. The move reveals how AI-driven demand is reshaping institutional real estate capital flows.

news:news.news:title: Starwood's $10.2B Fund Shows Data Center Capital Is Too Big for Traditional CRE Allocations
news:news.news:keywords: Starwood Capital, data centers, opportunistic fund, Barry Sternlicht, institutional capital, AI infrastructure, capital allocation

item.title: Kennedy Wilson-Jamison JV Shows Affordable Housing Capital Is Shifting from Subsidy to Structure
item.description: Kennedy Wilson and Jamison form JV to deliver 4,000 affordable units in L.A. via adaptive reuse. The deal signals a capital shift toward private equity structures over subsidy-dependent models.

news:news.news:title: Kennedy Wilson-Jamison JV Shows Affordable Housing Capital Is Shifting from Subsidy to Structure
news:news.news:keywords: Kennedy Wilson, Jamison, affordable housing, adaptive reuse, Los Angeles, Vintage Housing, Arden Residential, joint venture, capital markets

item.title: Pacific Park's $5B Plan: The State Is the Only Developer Left With Patient Capital
item.description: Pacific Park's $5B second phase plan shows state-led development absorbing risk private capital won't touch. 5,600 units, 1,242 affordable, new developer team.

news:news.news:title: Pacific Park's $5B Plan: The State Is the Only Developer Left With Patient Capital
news:news.news:keywords: Pacific Park, Brooklyn development, affordable housing, state-led development, capital markets

item.title: BridgeCity's $72M LIC Loan Shows Construction Debt Is Back, but Only for the Right Basis
item.description: BridgeCity Capital lends $72.3M for Elmord Management's 161-unit LIC development. The $28M land basis and private lender structure reveal where construction debt is flowing in 2026.

news:news.news:title: BridgeCity's $72M LIC Loan Shows Construction Debt Is Back, but Only for the Right Basis
news:news.news:keywords: BridgeCity Capital, Elmord Management, Long Island City, construction financing, private credit

item.title: APS's €3.3B Target Signals Banks Are Ready to Sell CRE Pain at Scale
item.description: APS Holding eyes €3.3B in portfolio deals as banks begin offloading distressed CRE debt. The scale signals a shift from one-off workouts to bulk loan sales.

news:news.news:title: APS's €3.3B Target Signals Banks Are Ready to Sell CRE Pain at Scale
news:news.news:keywords: distressed debt, portfolio sales, bank balance sheets, European CRE, APS Holding, interest rates, liquidity

item.title: Sonder's Collapse Exposes the Risk in Short-Term Rental CMBS Structures
item.description: Moinian Group's $131.5M CMBS loan on 2 Washington Street hits special servicing after Sonder's bankruptcy, exposing the fragility of master-lease structures in securitized debt.

news:news.news:title: Sonder's Collapse Exposes the Risk in Short-Term Rental CMBS Structures
news:news.news:keywords: CMBS, special servicing, Moinian Group, Sonder, short-term rentals, master lease, distress

item.title: Sergey Brin's 94% Loss on NYC Rent-Stabilized Fund Shows the Sector's Capital Trap
item.description: Sergey Brin sold his A&E Real Estate stake at a 94% loss. The $79 million exit reveals how rent stabilization, debt, and violations have destroyed equity value in NYC multifamily.

news:news.news:title: Sergey Brin's 94% Loss on NYC Rent-Stabilized Fund Shows the Sector's Capital Trap
news:news.news:keywords: rent stabilization, NYC multifamily, distressed equity, A&E Real Estate, Sergey Brin

item.title: Fed Rate-Hike Bets Signal CRE's Next Capital Squeeze
item.description: Traders are betting on a July Fed rate hike. For CRE, the risk is not the 25 bps but the liquidity freeze it triggers in debt markets already under pressure.

news:news.news:title: Fed Rate-Hike Bets Signal CRE's Next Capital Squeeze
news:news.news:keywords: Federal Reserve, interest rates, CRE capital markets, liquidity, refinancing risk

item.title: Charles Cohen's $204M Counterclaim: The Foreclosure Process Is the Next Battleground
item.description: Charles Cohen seeks $204M in damages from Fortress over UCC foreclosure auction. The fight exposes how foreclosure process can become a liability for lenders.

news:news.news:title: Charles Cohen's $204M Counterclaim: The Foreclosure Process Is the Next Battleground
news:news.news:keywords: Charles Cohen, Fortress Investment Group, UCC foreclosure, distressed debt, commercial real estate litigation, New York office, lender liability

item.title: Soloviev's $1.8B Refi Shows CMBS Is Open, But Only for the Right Trophy
item.description: Soloviev's $1.8B CMBS refi of 9 West 57th Street leads May's $3.1B in NYC loans. Apollo backs RXR conversion. Market signals revealed.

news:news.news:title: Soloviev's $1.8B Refi Shows CMBS Is Open, But Only for the Right Trophy
news:news.news:keywords: CMBS, Soloviev Group, 9 West 57th Street, RXR, Apollo Global Management, office conversion, industrial refinancing, Manhattan office, Billionaires' Row, Ladder Capital

item.title: Blackstone's $3.5B Data Center Sale Shows the Exit, Not the Thesis
item.description: Blackstone sells three NoVA data centers to Digital Realty for $3.5B. The cash-and-stock deal shows how AI infrastructure capital cycles from development to permanent ownership.

news:news.news:title: Blackstone's $3.5B Data Center Sale Shows the Exit, Not the Thesis
news:news.news:keywords: Blackstone, Digital Realty, data centers, Northern Virginia, AI infrastructure, capital recycling, joint venture

item.title: Real Capital Solutions' $49.5M Atlanta Buy Shows Office Repricing Has a Floor, Not a Ceiling
item.description: Real Capital Solutions pays $49.5M for 101 Marietta in downtown Atlanta at $73/sf. The 54%-leased tower shows value-add office capital is back, but only at deeply repriced bases.

news:news.news:title: Real Capital Solutions' $49.5M Atlanta Buy Shows Office Repricing Has a Floor, Not a Ceiling
news:news.news:keywords: Atlanta office, Real Capital Solutions, office repricing, value-add, Centennial Yards, distressed office

item.title: Waterton's $81M Buy Shows Multifamily Capital Is Chasing Basis, Not Yield
item.description: Waterton pays $80.5M for a 358-unit Pembroke Pines property at $225K/unit, a modest premium over 2021. The deal signals institutional capital is prioritizing defensible basis over yield in South Florida.

news:news.news:title: Waterton's $81M Buy Shows Multifamily Capital Is Chasing Basis, Not Yield
news:news.news:keywords: Waterton, multifamily, South Florida, basis, institutional capital, Pembroke Pines, Bar Invest

item.title: A Landlord's Foreclosure and a Rent Freeze: Capital, Politics, and the Unwind of Signature Bank's Book
item.description: A $36M foreclosure tied to former Signature Bank loans shows how political risk, rent regulation, and bankruptcy are reshaping the capital stack for NYC rent-stabilized housing.

news:news.news:title: A Landlord's Foreclosure and a Rent Freeze: Capital, Politics, and the Unwind of Signature Bank's Book
news:news.news:keywords: Signature Bank, foreclosure, rent stabilization, NYC, political risk, Santander, distressed debt

item.title: Stern's Missed Payment Shows the Capital Stack Is Still Unwinding at 9 DeKalb
item.description: Michael Stern misses a $2M court-ordered deposit to Silverstein Capital, exposing the unresolved capital stack tension at Brooklyn's tallest tower.

news:news.news:title: Stern's Missed Payment Shows the Capital Stack Is Still Unwinding at 9 DeKalb
news:news.news:keywords: Michael Stern, Silverstein Capital, 9 DeKalb Avenue, mezzanine debt, distress

item.title: Freddie Mac's 2026 Underwriting: Coverage Is Manufactured, Not Earned
item.description: Freddie Mac's 2026 K-series shows DSCRs of 1.41x rely on IO periods. Strip the IO, and coverage falls near 1.2x. A market buying time, not cash flow.

news:news.news:title: Freddie Mac's 2026 Underwriting: Coverage Is Manufactured, Not Earned
news:news.news:keywords: Freddie Mac, multifamily, underwriting, agency lending, debt service coverage, interest-only, CRE capital markets

item.title: Truist's $300M IOS Facility Shows Bank Appetite Is Sector-Specific, Not Broad
item.description: Truist Bank provides a $300 million acquisition facility to JIOS for industrial outdoor storage. The deal reveals where regional banks are willing to lend and why.

news:news.news:title: Truist's $300M IOS Facility Shows Bank Appetite Is Sector-Specific, Not Broad
news:news.news:keywords: Truist Bank, JIOS, Jadian Capital, industrial outdoor storage, acquisition facility, Cooper-Horowitz, regional bank lending

item.title: Citi's $44.5M CMBS Loan Shows Multifamily Refinancing Is About Basis, Not Confidence
item.description: Citigroup provides $44.5M CMBS loan for Benchmark's East Village multifamily. The refi at 103% of purchase price shows how basis and sponsor quality unlock liquidity.

news:news.news:title: Citi's $44.5M CMBS Loan Shows Multifamily Refinancing Is About Basis, Not Confidence
news:news.news:keywords: CMBS, multifamily, refinancing, Citigroup, Benchmark Real Estate, East Village, Manhattan, JLL

item.title: AES Buyout Vote Reveals Infrastructure's New Capital Rule: Scale Wins, Public Markets Lose
item.description: AES shareholders approve $10.7B acquisition by GIP/BlackRock, EQT, CalPERS, and QIA. The 97.9% vote reveals private capital's pricing power and public market discount for infrastructure assets.

news:news.news:title: AES Buyout Vote Reveals Infrastructure's New Capital Rule: Scale Wins, Public Markets Lose
news:news.news:keywords: AES, Global Infrastructure Partners, BlackRock, EQT, CalPERS, Qatar Investment Authority, infrastructure, take-private, capital markets, private equity

item.title: Greystar's $3B Fund Target Shows Multifamily Capital Is Concentrating, Not Expanding
item.description: Greystar has raised $1.5B in six months for its 12th US value-add multifamily fund, targeting $3B. The signal: LP capital is concentrating with the largest operators, not spreading across the market.

news:news.news:title: Greystar's $3B Fund Target Shows Multifamily Capital Is Concentrating, Not Expanding
news:news.news:keywords: Greystar, multifamily, value-add, fundraising, LP capital, institutional real estate

item.title: Supreme Court's Cook Ruling Buys CRE Time, Not Certainty, on Rate Path
item.description: Supreme Court blocks Trump's firing of Fed Governor Lisa Cook, preserving current rate policy. CRE owners face continued refinancing pressure as dovish pivot delayed.

news:news.news:title: Supreme Court's Cook Ruling Buys CRE Time, Not Certainty, on Rate Path
news:news.news:keywords: Federal Reserve, interest rates, monetary policy, refinancing risk, commercial real estate
